## CONTEXT

- **Project:** Super Flashcards 🟡
- **Current version:** v3.0.2
- **Target version:** v3.1.0
- **Production URL:** https://learn.rentyourcio.com
- **DB:** Cloud SQL 35.224.242.223, instance flashcards-db, DB LanguageLearning
- **DB login:** dedicated SF login (see PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md for credentials via Secret Manager)

**Why this sprint:**
SF has accumulated CRUD bugs blocking normal usage. SF-020 (delete button broken) is the only open P1. Several P2 items in executing/backlog status need to advance. The membership model (SF-005) was closed prematurely and needs to be reopened and properly implemented after CRUD is stable. This sprint clears the CRUD debt and delivers the membership foundation.

**STOP. Post Phase 0 findings before writing any code.** Include:
- Checkpoint codes from all docs
- Current version confirmed
- Full list of open SF requirements with current status
- Any CRUD endpoints that return errors
- Assessment: do the requirements below match MetaPM state? Flag any discrepancies.

---

## REQUIREMENTS

### SF-020 — Delete Button Broken (P1)
**Problem:** Delete button on flashcard/deck does not function. PL cannot delete cards or decks from the UI.
**Suspected cause:** Verify in Phase 0 — likely a missing DELETE route, a broken event handler, or a CSRF/auth issue on the delete endpoint.
**Implementation guidance:**
- Verify DELETE endpoints exist for `/api/decks/{id}` and `/api/cards/{id}`
- Verify frontend delete button wiring (event handler calls correct endpoint)
- Verify auth is not blocking DELETE method
- Test: create a deck, create a card, delete both from UI, confirm gone from DB
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Delete button visible on deck view
- [ ] Delete button visible on card view
- [ ] Clicking delete removes item from UI immediately
- [ ] Item is gone from DB (verify via GET after delete)
- [ ] No 404/500 errors in browser console on delete
- [ ] Deleting a deck deletes all its cards (cascade, verify in DB)

---

### SF-005 — Membership Model (P2, re-open)
**Problem:** SF-005 was marked closed but the membership model was not fully implemented. After SF-020 is stable, implement the membership foundation.
**Background:** SF needs to support multiple users with per-user deck ownership. Currently all data is either shared or tied to a single implicit user. The membership model gates deck access per authenticated user.
**Suspected scope (verify against PK.md in Phase 0):**
- User table exists or needs creation
- Deck table needs `owner_user_id` foreign key if not present
- All deck CRUD endpoints must enforce ownership (user can only read/write their own decks)
- Card endpoints inherit deck ownership rules
**Implementation guidance:**
- Check current schema in Phase 0. Report what exists.
- Do NOT break existing data. Migration must preserve all current decks.
- If user table does not exist, create it with: id, email, display_name, created_at, is_active
- Add `owner_user_id` to decks table (nullable initially to preserve existing data)
- Enforce ownership in API layer: GET /api/decks returns only calling user's decks
- Use existing OAuth/auth token to identify user (check PK.md for current auth approach)
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Schema migration runs without errors
- [ ] Existing decks preserved after migration
- [ ] GET /api/decks returns only the authenticated user's decks
- [ ] POST /api/decks creates deck owned by authenticated user
- [ ] User cannot DELETE another user's deck (returns 403)
- [ ] User cannot GET another user's deck (returns 403 or 404)
- [ ] Health endpoint shows v3.1.0

---

### SF-007 — Spaced Repetition Algorithm (P2, executing)
**Problem:** Spaced repetition is in executing status but may not be fully shipped. Verify in Phase 0.
**Implementation guidance:**
- Phase 0: check if SF-007 is actually deployed or just started. If already deployed and working, skip. If partially done, complete it.
- Standard SM-2 algorithm: after each card review, update `next_review_at` and `ease_factor` fields
- GET /api/cards/due — returns cards due for review today for the authenticated user
- Review endpoint: POST /api/cards/{id}/review with body `{"result": "pass"|"fail"}`
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Cards table has `next_review_at`, `ease_factor`, `review_count` fields
- [ ] POST /api/cards/{id}/review updates scheduling fields
- [ ] GET /api/cards/due returns only cards past their `next_review_at`
- [ ] Failed cards resurface sooner than passed cards (verify with two test cards)

---

### SF-013 — PIE Root Display (P2, executing)
**Problem:** PIE (Proto-Indo-European) root display is in executing status. Verify in Phase 0.
**Implementation guidance:**
- Phase 0: check current status. If deployed, verify it works. If not, implement.
- Cards should optionally display a PIE root field when present
- PIE root data comes from Etymython integration (see PK.md for current integration status)
- If Etymython integration is not ready, add PIE root as a manual text field on cards for now
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Card detail view shows PIE root field when populated
- [ ] PIE root field is optional (cards without it display normally)
- [ ] PIE root is editable in card edit view

---

## IMPLEMENTATION ORDER

1. **Phase 0 diagnostic** — report before anything else
2. **SF-020 delete fix** — P1, unblocks PL from basic usage. Deploy after.
3. **SF-007 spaced repetition** — if not deployed. Deploy after.
4. **SF-013 PIE root** — if not deployed. Deploy after.
5. **SF-005 membership model** — last, as it touches schema and all CRUD paths. Deploy after.
6. **Final deploy + health check** — confirm v3.1.0

Deploy after each phase. Do not batch. If time-limited, complete in priority order and stop cleanly with SESSION_CLOSEOUT.md.

## TEST COMMANDS

Run these against production after each deploy:

```powershell
# Health check — confirm version
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://learn.rentyourcio.com/health"
# Expected: {"version": "3.1.0", ...}

# Decks list
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://learn.rentyourcio.com/api/decks"

# Cards due
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://learn.rentyourcio.com/api/cards/due"

# Full smoke test (deck → card → review → delete)
# 1. POST /api/decks — create test deck, capture id
# 2. POST /api/cards — create test card in deck
# 3. POST /api/cards/{id}/review — submit review result
# 4. DELETE /api/cards/{id} — delete card, verify 200
# 5. DELETE /api/decks/{id} — delete deck, verify 200
# 6. GET /api/decks — confirm deck is gone
```

## METAPM HANDOFF

After completing the sprint, post results to MetaPM:

```powershell
Invoke-RestMethod -Method POST `
  -Uri "https://metapm.rentyourcio.com/api/uat/submit" `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body '{
    "project": "Super Flashcards",
    "version": "3.1.0",
    "feature": "SF-MS1: CRUD Bugs + Membership",
    "status": "passed",
    "total_tests": 0,
    "results_text": "<CC fills in — list each requirement, pass/fail, any notes>",
    "results": [],
    "tested_by": "cc",
    "notes": "SF-020, SF-005, SF-007, SF-013"
  }'
```

## SESSION CLOSE-OUT

1. Create `SESSION_CLOSEOUT.md` in project root with:
   - Sprint ID: SF-MS1
   - Version: v3.0.2 → v3.1.0
   - Requirements completed (list codes)
   - Requirements deferred (list codes + reason)
   - Commit hashes
   - Cloud Run revision name
   - Lessons learned (process/methodology only — not bug descriptions)
   - Open issues for next session
   - Next recommended action

2. Update `PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md`:
   - New version
   - Schema changes (if any)
   - New endpoints added
   - Membership model design decisions
   - Any patterns discovered

3. Commit and push:
```powershell
git add -A
git commit -m "v3.1.0: CRUD fix + membership model [SF-020, SF-005, SF-007, SF-013]"
git push origin main
```

4. Deploy and verify:
```powershell
# Use deploy workaround:
gcloud config set account cprator@cbsware.com
gcloud run deploy super-flashcards --source . --region us-central1
# Switch back:
gcloud config set account cc-deploy@super-flashcards-475210.iam.gserviceaccount.com
# Verify:
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://learn.rentyourcio.com/health"
```

---

## RULES

- Deploy to Cloud Run and test against production. No local validation.
- Never prompt for passwords. Use Secret Manager for all credentials.
- Never create virtual environments.
- If a command fails, report the error and try an alternative. Do NOT silently exit.
- Phase 0 is MANDATORY. Stop and report before writing code.
- Read PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE.md before writing any code.
- Copy UAT_Template_v3.html for UAT — never recreate it.
- Do not paraphrase metapm_descriptions_seed.json canonical descriptions.
- If scope exceeds estimated hours, complete in priority order and stop cleanly.

---

## NOTE TO CC

The membership model (SF-005) is the highest-risk item in this sprint. It touches schema and all CRUD paths. If you run short on time, deliver SF-020 + SF-007 + SF-013 first and defer SF-005 with a clean closeout. A partial-but-stable v3.1.0 is better than a broken membership migration.

If Phase 0 reveals that SF-007 or SF-013 are already fully deployed and working, skip them and use that time on SF-005.
